description: Ontology for deterministic analysis of CAC, Payback Period, LTV/CAC ratio, and segmented acquisition economics. A CTO who doesn't understand CAC decides architecture without understanding margins.

concepts.name: Customer Acquisition Cost
concepts.formula: (Total Marketing Spend + Total Sales Spend) / New Paying Customers
concepts.description: The total cost to acquire one paying customer. Must include: salaries (marketing + sales), commissions, tools (CRM, HubSpot), content, events, agencies, demos, SDRs, paid ads, marketing automation. Must NOT include: support, product, infrastructure.

concepts.name: CAC Payback Period
concepts.formula: CAC / (Monthly Revenue per Customer * Gross Margin %)
concepts.description: Months to recover the investment in acquiring a customer. Excellent: < 12 months. Acceptable: 12-18. Dangerous: > 18. Unsustainable: > 24.

concepts.name: Lifetime Value to CAC Ratio
concepts.description: The ratio investors actually examine. Below 1 = dead. 1-3 = mediocre. 3-5 = healthy. > 5 = either brilliant or under-investing in growth.

concepts.name: Segmented CAC Analysis
concepts.description: CAC must be measured per channel, per industry, per country, and per customer size. Enterprise may have CAC 12k but LTV 120k. SMB may have CAC 800 but brutal churn.

concepts.name: Gross Margin
concepts.description: The percentage of revenue remaining after direct costs. SaaS target: 70-85%. Below 60% signals infrastructure inefficiency or pricing problems.

concepts.name: Blended vs Paid CAC
concepts.description: Founders often report blended CAC (mixing organic + paid). Paid-only CAC is always higher and more honest. Must separate to avoid self-deception.
